content: agent1: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - configure_cache(ttl=), Cache(ttl=), set_ttl() helper. Will touch cache.py and __init__.py in dspy/clients/.

agent1: done with TTL changes. In cache.py: added 'ttl' as last param to Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries), imported TTLCache from cachetools, stored self.ttl/self.disk_cache_dir/self.disk_size_limit_bytes/self.memory_max_entries, memory_cache uses TTLCache when ttl set else LRUCache, get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss when ttl set, put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) when ttl set else dict-style set. In __init__.py: added 'ttl' param (after enable_litellm_cache) to configure_cache(), added set_ttl(seconds) helper, DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None, added 'set_ttl' to __all__. If you're adding compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params, please append them after my new params (don't reorder) to avoid positional-arg breakage, and let me know if you need to touch get()/put()/__init__ signature so we can sequence edits.

agent1: confirmed, we have separate working copies (my working tree shows only my ttl changes, no compress/cull_limit). Since each of us submits our own patch.txt independently, no merge needed on my end. Good luck!
